mechanism_narrative: A higher level of the federal funds rate mechanically raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, reducing present equity values and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward returns is positive or insignificantly negative with p-value above 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals emerging labor market slack that reduces household income growth and consumer spending, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of triggered months when UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Falling real GDP alongside elevated CPI inflation compresses corporate margins through a stagflationary channel, and the interaction of these two variables predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL fails to show a statistically significant negative coefficient at the 5 percent level when predicting 60-day forward returns, or the combined signal underperforms a single-proxy CPIAUCSL model.

mechanism_narrative: In an accommodative monetary policy environment, month-over-month increases in industrial production transmit to positive earnings revisions without offsetting discount rate fears, causing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with FEDFUNDS below 2.5 percent
failure_condition: During periods where FEDFUNDS is below 2.5 percent, month-over-month increases in INDPRO do not predict positive 20-day SPX forward returns with a t-test p-value below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in industrial production indicate a contracting supply side that reduces aggregate corporate revenues and operating leverage, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month changes in INDPRO show a non-negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 5-day SPX forward returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1300 thousand units signal durable goods demand and construction sector strength that spills over into broad economic activity, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1300, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative on average or the positive hit rate falls below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly accelerating payroll employment and rising consumer prices signal an overheating labor market that pressures the Federal Reserve to tighten policy abruptly, and this interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL fails to significantly predict negative 20-day SPX returns at the 5 percent level or produces a Sharpe ratio below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales below 500000 million dollars signal weakening consumer demand that leads to negative earnings surprises and de-rating of the S&P 500, producing negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When RSAFS is below 500000, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of observations or the mean return is not significantly negative.
